description_long: This is a roughly circular, deep bowl with a visibly uneven rim and a coarse, matte surface texture. The bowl appears to be made from a single piece of stone or ceramic, with a grayish-brown coloration and a slightly irregular, hand-formed shape. There are several chips and notches along the rim, indicating signs of wear or age. The interior is smooth but not polished, matching the exterior in color and finish. A small, rectangular label is affixed to the inner rim, suggesting cataloging or museum use. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no visible decorative patterns or embellishments.
description: A grayish-brown, roughly circular stone or ceramic bowl with a chipped, uneven rim and a coarse, matte finish.
description_view_0: This view shows the bowl's interior and highlights a large chip on the rim and a white label affixed to the inner edge.
description_view_1: This angle reveals another chipped section of the rim and a second label on the exterior, emphasizing the bowl's irregular shape.
description_view_2: This side displays a smoother rim section with fewer chips, showing the bowl's depth and consistent coloration.
description_view_3: This perspective focuses on the opposite side of the bowl, again showing the interior and a prominent rim chip near the label.
attribute_context_of_use: holding or serving food, or as a historical artifact

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
